CASE DISMISSED AS TO COMPLAINT OF LAURA LATVA IN ITS ENTIRETY BY COURT AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING JAN-30-2006 AT 1:30PM. THE COURT FINDS NO GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PREVIOUS COURT ORDERS. A LESS SEVERE SANCTION WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE DUE TO THE HISTORY OF LACK OF COMPLIANCE. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: HELEN SOREP-MATLEY, CSR	225. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LATVA, LAURA TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT O'CONNOR, MICHAEL E DBA JUSTICE LEAGUE (AFTER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS) SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2006 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2006 Continued To Mar-27-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Jennifer L. Sommer, Esq., appearing for Defendat. The OSC is re-set to 3/27/2006 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. At the end of the Calendar court re-called the Case. There being no appearance by the Plaintiff in Propria Persona. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substancial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, It is hereby ordered that the Complaint is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Court ordered an OSC to Cross-Complainant on 3/27/2006 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225.
